user_prompt: Use argparse, allow the user to specify any `*.wav` file as the input, and an output path. If the output path is not specified, then the output path is the same place as the `*.wav` file but with an extension like `*.trans.json`
Assume `OPENAI_API_KEY` is already set in the environment
Use OpenAI’s API platform to perform the transcription.
agent_reply: It defaults output to `input.trans.json`, reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from the environment, uploads the WAV to OpenAI’s `/v1/audio/transcriptions` endpoint, and writes the returned JSON with pretty formatting. Default model is `gpt-4o-transcribe`, with `--model`, `--language`, `--prompt`, and `--response-format` options.
I didn’t run an actual transcription call. Official API references used: OpenAI transcription endpoint and `gpt-4o-transcribe` model docs:
